title: remove image failed to write a file
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I got a gnome notification about low disk space, so I went straight to podman to remove some images.  `podman rmi` reported an error because it could not write a file. It's unintuitive that it needs to write a file to delete images. If podman *requires* free disk space in order to delete images, that would be less than ideal.

But in this case, the image did go away (it was no longer listed in `podman images`). So it's unclear if this failure really mattered, or if this is the sort of internal error that doesn't need to be reported to the user.

```
$ podman rmi 18776ffc8c20
Error: write /home/mhrivnak/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-layers/.tmp-layers.json3185203766: no space left on device
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run out of disk space while some background process is writing data (it looked like flatpak-system-helper was writing 100s of MBs of data in the background for unknown reasons)

2.  delete an image with podman

**Describe the results you received:**

The image did get deleted (it was no longer present in the list of `podman images`), but I got the error message reported above.

**Describe the results you expected:**

image goes away and I get back disk space without any errors


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Fri Nov 11 10:24:13 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Yes this is the same issue.
